title: Ensure that the admission control policy is not set to AlwaysAdmit

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control policy is not set to AlwaysAdmit
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to AlwaysAdmit allows all requests and do not filter any requests.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the master node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --admission-control argument is set to a value that does not include AlwaysAdmit .
input_row.remediation: Edit the /etc/kubernetes/apiserver file on the master node and set the KUBE_ADMISSION_CONTROL parameter to a value that does not include AlwaysAdmit . Based on your system, restart the kube-apiserver service. For example: systemctl restart kube-apiserver.service Impact: Only requests explicitly allowed by the admissions control policy would be served.
input_row.default_value: By default, AlwaysAdmit is used if no --admission-control flag is provided.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_control_policy_not_always_admit

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].args}'` to retrieve the arguments passed to the kube-apiserver.
- Specifically, check for the `--admission-control` flag in the output.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** The `--admission-control` argument does not include `AlwaysAdmit`.
  - **Fail:** The `--admission-control` argument includes `AlwaysAdmit` or is not set at all (default behavior).
- This check ensures that the admission control policy is configured to restrict requests appropriately, preventing all requests from being accepted.
